name: Fortuna and Embalse de Santomera Loop
name_original: Baños de Fortuna y Embalse de Santomera — vuelta desde Abanilla
description: This difficult 24.8-mile touring cycling route from Abanilla explores the scenic Santomera Reservoir and the thermal springs of Los Baños de
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Abanilla traverse a diverse landscape in the northeast of Murcia, Spain. The region features arid "badlands" with dramatic geological formations, contrasting with fertile river valleys like the River Chícamo. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, including elevated paths in the Sierra de El Cantón and areas with palm, almond, and olive trees. This blend of semi-desert and lush areas provides a range of experiences for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Abanilla The most popular touring cycling route is Fortuna and Embalse de Santomera Loop, a 24.8 miles (39.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic Santomera Reservoir and the thermal springs of Los Baños de Fortuna. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Los Baños de Fortuna – Desierto de Abanilla loop from Los Baños, a difficult 23.9 miles (38.5 km) path. This route offers views of the striking "lunar landscape" of the Desierto de Abanilla. Local touring cyclists also love the Los Baños de Fortuna – Desert Landscape near Murcia loop from Los Baños, a 11.0 miles (17.7 km) trail leading through desert landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Abanilla is defined by arid badlands, fertile river valleys, and mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Abanilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Abanilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Abanilla?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Abanilla, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Abanilla?
A: Abanilla offers a striking contrast of landscapes. You'll cycle through arid "badlands" with dramatic geological formations, often described as a "lunar landscape," and alongside fertile river valleys like the River Chícamo, featuring palm, almond, and olive trees. Elevated paths in the Sierra de El Cantón also provide different terrain and panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Abanilla?
A: Given the region's generally warm climate, spring and autumn are typically the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Abanilla. The temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable rides through both the arid badlands and the river valleys. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, so checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Abanilla offers routes for various fitness levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are at least 3 easy touring cycling routes available. Additionally, 23 moderate routes provide a good balance for those with some cycling experience but not seeking extreme challenges. An example of a moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Baños de Fortuna – Desert Landscape near Murcia loop from Los Baños&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Abanilla are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017136884/fortuna-and-embalse-de-santomera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortuna and Embalse de Santomera Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31519943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo-Segura Canal – Algueda Canal Path loop from Abanilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore along the touring cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the unique "lunar landscape" of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Abanilla&lt;/a&gt;, explore the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chicamo River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the intriguing salt formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6136157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rambla Salada&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable natural features include viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2760010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortao de las Peñas&lt;/a&gt; and even a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Final Waterfall of the Via Ferrata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to visit while cycling in the Abanilla region?
A: Absolutely. The historic town of Abanilla itself offers medieval narrow streets and traditional architecture, including the San José Baroque Church. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Baños de Fortuna&lt;/a&gt;, a small spa town known for its healing thermal springs. The Molino del Chícamo Interpretation Centre provides insights into the region's water supply history and natural beauty along the Chícamo River.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: While many routes in Abanilla are moderate to difficult, there are easier options that could be suitable for families, particularly those with some cycling experience. Look for the 3 routes classified as 'easy' in the guide, which typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances. Always check the specific route details on komoot for suitability with children.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: Parking availability varies by route. For routes exploring the Embalse de Santomera, you can often find parking near the reservoir. For routes venturing into the Desierto de Abanilla, trailheads near Los Baños de Fortuna or other access points may offer parking. It's recommended to check the starting point details of your chosen komoot route for specific parking information.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Abanilla using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Abanilla and surrounding areas like Los Baños de Fortuna exist, primarily via bus services. However, direct access to specific trailheads with a bicycle might require some planning. It's advisable to research local bus routes and their bicycle policies in advance if you plan to use public transport to reach your starting point.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the touring cycling routes in Abanilla?
A: Yes, you'll find opportunities for refreshments. The town of Abanilla itself has cafes and restaurants. The spa town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Baños de Fortuna&lt;/a&gt;, a common starting or passing point for many routes, also offers cafes and restaurants where you can refuel. For routes in more remote areas, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks.

Q: What is the 'Murcian Route 66' and does it pass through Abanilla?
A: The 'Murcian Route 66' is a network of trails designed for activities like cycling and hiking, known for its desert and badlands aesthetics. It does indeed pass through Abanilla, as well as Fortuna, Desiertos de Mahoya, and the Sierra de la Pila, offering a unique cycling experience through this distinctive landscape.

Q: What is the terrain like on the Fortuna and Embalse de Santomera Loop?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017136884/fortuna-and-embalse-de-santomera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortuna and Embalse de Santomera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering approximately 24.8 miles (39.9 km) with significant elevation changes (around 370m up and down). It explores the scenic Santomera Reservoir and the thermal springs of Los Baños de Fortuna, offering varied terrain that includes both paved and unpaved segments, requiring good fitness.
tips: The Abanilla Desert, also known as the Mahoya Desert, is one of the most unique and fascinating places in the Murcia region, just an hour's drive from the beaches of the Costa Cálida and Costa Blanca South. This surreal landscape, often compared to the surface of the moon, offers a perfect combination of natural beauty and tranquility. Whether you're a nature lover, an adventurer, or simply looking for a place to relax,

A small oasis in the middle of nowhere - this is the best way to describe the spa town of Los Baños de Fortuna with its world-famous, healing thermal springs. Seniors stroll through the streets in bathrobes while the setting sun bathes the village with its many palm trees in a wonderfully cozy evening light. There is also a small shop. It makes sense, however, not to think about what you would like to cook in advance, but to let yourself be inspired by the display, which is sometimes more spartan than lavish.

At this point we are at the Santomera Reservoir dam, which offers us incredible landscapes in an ideal environment for practicing any sport or photography. In the place there are several picnic areas where you can eat and / or cook. Without a doubt, one of the best places to disconnect from the city and connect with nature.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for touring cyclists. It requires good fitness, and you should be prepared for some challenging sections where you may even need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Banos de Fortuna y Embalse de Santomera — return from Abanilla trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, featuring a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. While some parts of the broader Santomera area have mostly paved roads, this specific trail includes unpaved and potentially difficult-to-ride segments, contributing to its challenging nature. You'll encounter well-rideable sandy paths, especially near the reservoir.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 39.9 kilometers long and typically takes about 2 hours and 38 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable points of interest. The route passes through the Desierto de Abanilla, and includes the picturesque Santomera Reservoir, known for its excellent views from ridgelines. You'll also visit Los Baños de Fortuna, a small oasis with world-famous healing thermal springs.

faqs.question: Are there good viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers stunning mountain scenery. Particularly, the ridgelines near the Santomera Reservoir provide excellent views, making it a scenic highlight of the ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the start in Abanilla are not provided, it's advisable to look for public parking areas within Abanilla town or near the initial segments of the route. As this is a return route from Abanilla, starting and ending there would be convenient for vehicle access.

faqs.question: Is the Banos de Fortuna y Embalse de Santomera — return from Abanilla trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: There is no specific information indicating restrictions on dogs for this touring bicycle route. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas and near any facilities or other users, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the climate in Murcia, the best times to cycle this route are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for cycling than the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the broader region of Humedal de Ajauque y Rambla Salada. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails in this natural area. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Los Baños de Fortuna, which is a small settlement known for its thermal springs. You may find some local establishments there. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on a difficult route like this, as services might be limited in other sections.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the name "Banos de Fortuna y Embalse de Santomera — return from Abanilla" indicates that this is a circular route, starting and ending in Abanilla.
